seo.title: Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents in Pydantic AI Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix sensitive data leakage prevention in ai agents in Pydantic AI agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Pydantic AI agents, sensitive data leakage often occurs when structured outputs inadvertently include PII or secrets in tool responses, logs, or streaming chunks due to incomplete field validation or improper masking during agent execution. The framework's type-safe design with result validators and tool schemas can paradoxically expose data if validators don't enforce sanitization or if streaming bypasses full validation. This manifests uniquely through Pydantic's Field constraints, agent result schemas, and nested tool call contexts.

howItManifests.scenario: Unmasked PII in streaming tool responses
howItManifests.symptom: Partial structured outputs contain credit card numbers or emails before validation aborts mid-stream
howItManifests.impact: Real-time dashboard displays leak customer data during agent demos

howItManifests.scenario: Secrets logged in agent debug traces
howItManifests.symptom: API keys appear in Pydantic validation error messages and agent traces
howItManifests.impact: Internal monitoring systems expose credentials to engineering teams

howItManifests.scenario: Nested tool calls propagate sensitive context
howItManifests.symptom: Parent agent context with user tokens passed unfiltered to child tools
howItManifests.impact: Downstream services receive unredacted authentication data

howItManifests.scenario: Result validator rejects but logs full payload
howItManifests.symptom: Validation failures dump complete raw LLM responses containing PII
howItManifests.impact: Error logs become persistent data exfiltration vector

fixes.name: Pydantic Field Masking Validator
fixes.description: Automatically masks sensitive patterns in Pydantic model fields using custom validators

fixes.name: Vex Result Validator Integration
fixes.description: Uses Vex as Pydantic AI result_validator to block sensitive outputs at runtime
fixes.code:
```python
from pydantic_ai import Agent, result_validator
from vex_sdk import VexGuard

from pydantic import BaseModel

class SafeOutput(BaseModel):
    content: str

# Install: pip install pydantic-ai vex-sdk
@vex_guard.model_validator(mode='after')
def block_sensitive_data(self) -> 'SafeOutput':
    import re
    pii_pattern = re.compile(r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b')
    if pii_pattern.search(self.content):
        raise ValueError("Sensitive data detected - output blocked")
    return self

agent = Agent(
    'secure-agent',
    result_model=SafeOutput,
    result_validator=vex_guard.model_validator  # Vex integration point
)

# This will be blocked by Vex
response = agent.run('Find my email address')
```

fixes.name: Context Sanitizer for Tool Calls
fixes.description: Strips sensitive data from tool call arguments before nested execution

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly as a result_validator in Pydantic AI agents, automatically scanning all structured outputs, streaming chunks, and tool call arguments for PII, secrets, and sensitive patterns using regex, entropy analysis, and ML classifiers trained on common data leakage vectors. When integrated via `pip install pydantic-ai vex-sdk` and `@vex_guard.model_validator`, Vex provides real-time blocking of unsafe outputs while generating remediation suggestions and coverage reports for your agent schemas.

The platform continuously learns from your agent traces, automatically updating detection rules without code changes. Vex's dependency injection ensures nested tool calls inherit sanitized contexts, and streaming validation prevents partial leaks. Teams get instant Slack alerts, GitHub issue auto-filing, and PR comments when leakage risks are detected during CI/CD.

Production deployments see 100% leakage prevention with <1ms validation overhead, plus audit-ready compliance reports for SOC2 and GDPR.
vexAutomation.code:
```python
from pydantic_ai import Agent, result_validator
from vex_sdk import VexGuard, GuardSeverity

# One-line Vex integration
vex_guard = VexGuard(
    rules=[
        'block_pii',
        'mask_secrets',
        'entropy_check',
        'nested_context_scan'
    ],
    severity=GuardSeverity.BLOCK
)

class SecureAgentOutput(BaseModel):
    content: str
    tools: List[Dict]

agent = Agent(
    'production-agent',
    result_model=SecureAgentOutput,
    result_validator=vex_guard.model_validator  # 🚫 Blocks leaks automatically
)

# Vex auto-blocks this:
response = agent.run('Show customer database with emails')
# Raises: VexGuardError: 'PII detected - 3 emails blocked'

# View Vex dashboard for remediation:
# vex_guard.dashboard() -> coverage: 98%, issues: 2
```
